name: Homemade Yogurt
recipeInstructions: There are several yogurt-making devices, but you can easily make it at home in a large thermos bottle using a candy thermometer. Sterilize all equipment in boiling water before use. Bring the milk to a boil, then cool to 100 degrees Fahrenheit. In a small bowl, blend the yogurt with 1/4 cup of warm milk. Whisk it back into the warm milk. Pour into a pre-warmed thermos, seal, and set aside in a warm place for 7 hours. Turn off heat by setting yogurt out on ice water bath to quicken cooling. Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours, or until thickened. Sweeten to taste if desired, and serve with raspberries. Yogurt will keep for 4 to 5 days when covered and refrigerated.
